options.text_translated: A liability insurance policy covering third-party injuries and property damage for at least 1,000 days of minimum wage
options.text_official_en: An insurance policy that guarantees damages to third parties in their property and persons for at least 1,000 days of minimum wage

explanation_en: All non-public-transit vehicles on Jalisco roads must carry liability insurance covering at least 6,000 days of minimum wage in third-party damages (both property and bodily injury). This is not optional -- it is a legal requirement. Full-coverage is not mandated by law; the minimum is third-party liability at the 6,000-day threshold.
explanation_es: Todos los vehículos que no sean de transporte público en las vias de Jalisco deben tener un seguro de responsabilidad civil que cubra al menos 6,000 días de salario mínimo en danos a terceros (tanto en bienes como en personas). Esto no es opcional -- es un requisito legal. La ley no exige cobertura amplia; el mínimo es responsabilidad civil con el umbral de 6,000 días.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: DE ACUERDO A LA PRELACIÓN DE PASO QUE SE CONTEMPLA EN EL REGLAMENTO DE TRÁNSITO, ¿CUÁLES SON LAS MEDIDAS DE SEGURIDAD QUE DEBEN OBSERVAR LOS CONDUCTORES QUE ARRIBAN A UNA GLORIETA?
question_translated: According to the right-of-way rules, what must drivers do when entering a roundabout?
question_official_en: According to the right-of-way precedence contemplated in the Traffic Regulation, what are the safety measures that drivers arriving at a roundabout must observe?

options.text_original: Incorporarse a la glorieta a baja velocidad y alerta a los demás conductores
options.text_translated: Merge into the roundabout at low speed, alert to other drivers
options.text_official_en: Enter the roundabout at low speed and alert to other drivers

options.text_original: Incorporarse a la glorieta circulando a 40 km/h, tocando previamente el claxon para llamar la atención y evitar un accidente
options.text_translated: Merge into the roundabout at 40 km/h, honking beforehand to get attention and avoid an accident
options.text_official_en: Enter the roundabout driving at 40 km/h, previously honking the horn to call attention and avoid an accident

options.text_original: Hacer alto total antes de mezclarse con la circulación, dando preferencia a los que ya transitan en ella
options.text_translated: Come to a full stop before merging, yielding to vehicles already in the roundabout
options.text_official_en: Make a complete stop before merging with traffic, giving preference to those already circulating in it

explanation_en: In Jalisco, you must come to a FULL STOP before entering a roundabout and yield to all vehicles already circulating inside it. This is stricter than many countries where you only slow down and yield. The key rule: stop completely first, then merge when safe. Vehicles already in the roundabout always have the right of way.
explanation_es: En Jalisco, debes hacer un ALTO TOTAL antes de entrar a una glorieta y ceder el paso a todos los vehículos que ya circulan dentro de ella. Esto es más estricto que en muchos paises donde solo se reduce la velocidad y se cede el paso. La regla clave: detenerse completamente primero, luego incorporarse cuando sea seguro. Los vehículos que ya están en la glorieta siempre tienen preferencia de paso.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SI AL CONDUCIR SU VEHÍCULO USTED LLEGA A UN CRUCE Y LA LUZ ROJA DEL SEMÁFORO ES INTERMITENTE ¿QUÉ DEBE HACER?:
question_translated: If you reach an intersection and the traffic light is flashing red, what should you do?
question_official_en: If while driving your vehicle you arrive at a crossing and the red traffic light is flashing, what should you do?

options.text_original: Acelerar y cruzar rápidamente la calle
options.text_translated: Speed up and cross the street quickly
options.text_official_en: Accelerate and cross the street rapidly

options.text_original: Hacer alto total y continuar la circulación después de cerciorarse que no se aproximen otros vehículos por la arteria transversal
options.text_translated: Come to a full stop, then proceed after confirming no vehicles are approaching from the cross street
options.text_official_en: Make a complete stop and continue driving after verifying that no other vehicles are approaching on the cross street

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad antes de entrar en la intersección y continuar con las precauciones necesarias
options.text_translated: Slow down before the intersection and continue with necessary caution
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed before entering the intersection and continue with necessary precautions

explanation_en: A flashing red light works exactly like a stop sign: you must come to a FULL STOP, look both ways, and then proceed only when it is safe. Do not confuse this with a flashing yellow light, which means slow down and use caution. Flashing red = stop sign behavior.
explanation_es: Una luz roja intermitente funciona exactamente como una señal de alto: debes hacer un ALTO TOTAL, mirar en ambas direcciones, y luego continuar solo cuando sea seguro. No confunda esto con una luz amarilla intermitente, que significa reducir velocidad y proceder con precaución. Roja intermitente = comportamiento de señal de alto.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: EN EL SEMÁFORO, ¿CUÁL ES EL SIGNIFICADO DE LA LUZ ÁMBAR O AMARILLA CONOCIDA TAMBIÉN COMO PREVENTIVA?:
question_translated: What does the yellow (amber) traffic light mean?
question_official_en: On the traffic light, what is the meaning of the amber or yellow light, also known as the preventive light?

options.text_original: El lapso que existe para despejar la intersección cuando algún vehículo haya entrado a ella o se encuentre muy próximo y no le sea posible detenerse inmediatamente por el riesgo de producirse un impacto por alcance
options.text_translated: It is the window of time to clear the intersection if you have already entered it, or if you are too close to stop safely without risking a rear-end collision
options.text_official_en: The time period that exists to clear the intersection when a vehicle has entered it or is very close and cannot stop immediately due to the risk of a rear-end impact

options.text_original: Que usted debe aumentar la velocidad para prevenir un accidente
options.text_translated: You should speed up to prevent an accident
options.text_official_en: That you should increase speed to prevent an accident

options.text_original: Que usted debe de utilizar el claxon y avanzar
options.text_translated: You should honk your horn and proceed
options.text_official_en: That you should use the horn and advance

explanation_en: The yellow/amber light gives you time to safely clear the intersection if you have already entered it or are too close to stop without causing a rear-end collision. It does NOT mean "speed up to beat the red." If you can safely stop before the intersection, you should. The yellow phase exists to prevent vehicles from being stranded mid-intersection when the light turns red.
explanation_es: La luz amarilla/ámbar te da tiempo para despejar la intersección de manera segura si ya entraste en ella o estás demasiado cerca para detenerte sin causar un choque por alcance. NO significa "acelera para ganarle al rojo." Si puedes detenerte de manera segura antes de la intersección, deberías hacerlo. La fase amarilla existe para evitar que los vehículos queden atrapados a mitad de la intersección cuando cambia a rojo.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SI EN UN CRUCERO EL SEMÁFORO LE INDICA “SIGA” PERO EL AGENTE VIAL LE INDICA A USTED “ALTO” ¿A QUIÉN DEBE DE OBEDECER?:
question_translated: If the traffic light says GO but the traffic officer signals you to STOP, who do you obey?
question_official_en: If at an intersection the traffic light indicates 'go' but the traffic officer indicates 'stop' to you, whom should you obey?

options.text_original: Al agente vial
options.text_translated: The traffic officer
options.text_official_en: The traffic officer

options.text_original: Al semáforo
options.text_translated: The traffic light
options.text_official_en: The traffic light

options.text_original: A los demás conductores
options.text_translated: The other drivers
options.text_official_en: The other drivers

explanation_en: A traffic officer ALWAYS overrides the traffic light. If there is a conflict between what the light shows and what the officer signals, you must obey the officer. Officers take manual control of intersections when there are emergencies, accidents, special events, or signal malfunctions. This is a universal rule in Mexico.
explanation_es: Un agente vial SIEMPRE tiene prioridad sobre el semáforo. Si hay un conflicto entre lo que muestra la luz y lo que senala el agente, debes obedecer al agente. Los agentes toman control manual de las intersecciones cuando hay emergencias, accidentes, eventos especiales o fallas en los semáforos. Está es una regla universal en Mexico.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁL ES LA DIFERENCIA ENTRE LAS SEÑALES DE TRÁNSITO RESTRICTIVAS Y LAS PREVENTIVAS?
question_translated: What is the difference between regulatory (restrictive) signs and warning (preventive) signs?
question_official_en: What is the difference between restrictive traffic signs and preventive traffic signs?

options.text_original: Además de sus colores, las señales restrictivas indican cierto tipo de limitaciones o prohibiciones con relación al tránsito vehicular y al uso de las vías públicas, mientras que las preventivas advierten la existencia o la naturaleza de algún peligro, o el cambio de situación en una vía pública
options.text_translated: Besides their colors, regulatory signs indicate limitations or prohibitions for vehicle traffic and public road use, while warning signs alert you to the presence or nature of a danger, or a change in road conditions
options.text_official_en: In addition to their colors, restrictive signs indicate certain types of limitations or prohibitions related to vehicular traffic and use of public roads, while preventive signs warn of the existence or nature of some danger, or a change of conditions on a public road

options.text_original: No existe diferencia alguna
options.text_translated: There is no difference
options.text_official_en: There is no difference whatsoever

options.text_original: La diferencia es su tamaño y también que las señales Restrictivas son de color azul con blanco y las preventivas verdes con blanco
options.text_translated: The difference is their size, and also that regulatory signs are blue with white and warning signs are green with white
options.text_official_en: The difference is their size and also that Restrictive signs are blue with white and preventive signs are green with white

explanation_en: Regulatory (restrictive) signs tell you what you CANNOT or MUST do -- speed limits, no-parking zones, one-way restrictions. Warning (preventive) signs alert you to upcoming hazards or changes -- curves, intersections, construction. They also differ in color: regulatory signs are typically red/white/black, while warning signs are black on yellow. Option C gives wrong colors.
explanation_es: Las señales restrictivas (reglamentarias) te indican lo que NO puedes o debes hacer -- límites de velocidad, zonas de no estacionarse, restricciones de sentido único. Las señales preventivas (de advertencia) te alertan sobre peligros o cambios próximos -- curvas, intersecciones, obras. También difieren en color: las restrictivas suelen ser rojo/blanco/negro, mientras que las preventivas son negro sobre amarillo. La opción C da colores incorrectos.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SEGÚN EL REGLAMENTO, TIENEN PRELACIÓN DE PASO EN VIALIDADES PRIMARIAS:
question_translated: According to the regulations, which roads have right-of-way priority on primary routes?
question_official_en: According to the regulation, primary roads have right-of-way precedence:

options.text_original: Las calles y calzadas
options.text_translated: Streets and causeways
options.text_official_en: Streets and causeways

options.text_original: Las calles, caminos y calzadas
options.text_translated: Streets, roads, and causeways
options.text_official_en: Streets, roads, and causeways

options.text_original: Avenidas, calzadas, viaductos y pares viales de primera magnitud, sobre todas las demás arterias
options.text_translated: Avenues, causeways, viaducts, and major paired one-way roads -- over all other streets
options.text_official_en: Avenues, causeways, viaducts, and major paired roads, over all other arterial roads

explanation_en: Primary roads with right-of-way priority in Jalisco are: avenues (avenidas), causeways (calzadas), viaducts (viaductos), and major paired one-way roads (pares viales de primera magnitud). These take priority over all smaller streets. If you are on a regular side street approaching an avenue, the avenue traffic has the right of way.
explanation_es: Las vialidades primarias con prelacion de paso en Jalisco son: avenidas, calzadas, viaductos y pares viales de primera magnitud. Estas tienen prioridad sobre todas las calles menores. Si tú está en una calle secundaria acercandose a una avenida, el tráfico de la avenida tiene la preferencia de paso.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICA UN CONDUCTOR CUANDO REALIZA ESTA SEÑAL HUMANA?:
question_translated: What is a driver indicating when making this hand signal? [IMAGE: shows driver hand signal]
question_official_en: What does a driver indicate when performing this human signal?

options.text_original: Alto
options.text_translated: Stop
options.text_official_en: Stop

options.text_original: Siga
options.text_translated: Proceed / Go
options.text_official_en: Continue

options.text_original: Vuelta a la izquierda
options.text_translated: Left turn
options.text_official_en: Left turn

explanation_en: This question references an image of a driver's hand signal. The correct answer is STOP. In Mexico (and internationally), when a driver extends their left arm out the window and bends it upward at the elbow with palm facing backward, or extends the arm straight out and angles it downward, it signals a stop or slow down to drivers behind.
explanation_es: Esta pregunta hace referencia a una imagen de una señal manual del conductor. La respuesta correcta es ALTO. En Mexico (e internacionalmente), cuando un conductor extiende su brazo izquierdo por la ventana y lo dobla hacia arriba en el codo con la palma hacia atras, o extiende el brazo recto y lo inclina hacia abajo, senala alto o reduccion de velocidad a los conductores detrás.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: LOS AGENTES DE VIALIDAD Y TRÁNSITO HACEN SEÑALES SONORAS CON SU SILBATO, ¿QUÉ SIGNIFICAN DOS SILBATAZOS CORTOS?:
question_translated: Traffic officers use whistle signals. What do two short whistle blasts mean?
question_official_en: Traffic and road officers make sound signals with their whistle. What do two short whistle blasts mean?

options.text_original: Siga
options.text_translated: Proceed / Go
options.text_official_en: Continue

options.text_original: Precaución
options.text_translated: Caution
options.text_official_en: Caution

options.text_original: Acelerar
options.text_translated: Speed up
options.text_official_en: Accelerate

explanation_en: Two short whistle blasts from a traffic officer mean PROCEED / GO. This is one of three standard whistle signals: one long blast = STOP, two short blasts = GO/PROCEED, and several short rapid blasts = CAUTION or attention. Memorize: 2 short = go.
explanation_es: Dos silbatazos cortos de un agente vial significan SIGA / AVANCE. Está es una de tres señales estándar con silbato: un silbatazo largo = ALTO, dos silbatazos cortos = SIGA, y varios silbatazos cortos y rapidos = Precaución o atención. Memorice: 2 cortos = avance.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: CUANDO LAS GUARNICIONES U ORILLAS DE LA ACERA SE ENCUENTRAN PINTADAS DE COLOR AMARILLO, ¿QUÉ INDICAN?:
question_translated: When curbs or sidewalk edges are painted yellow, what does that mean?
question_official_en: When the curbs or edges of the sidewalk are painted yellow, what do they indicate?

options.text_original: Que el lugar está reservado
options.text_translated: The spot is reserved
options.text_official_en: That the place is reserved

options.text_original: Exclusivo para vehículos oficiales
options.text_translated: Exclusive for official / government vehicles
options.text_official_en: Exclusive for official vehicles

options.text_original: Que el estacionamiento en ese lugar está prohibido
options.text_translated: Parking is prohibited there
options.text_official_en: That parking in that location is prohibited

explanation_en: Yellow-painted curbs in Jalisco mean NO PARKING. This is a clear, universal-style rule: if the curb is yellow, do not park there. It does not mean "reserved" or "government vehicles only" -- it is a flat prohibition on parking for everyone. Look for yellow curbs especially near intersections, fire hydrants, and bus stops.
explanation_es: Las guarniciones pintadas de amarillo en Jalisco significan PROHIBIDO ESTACIONARSE. Está es una regla clara y de estilo universal: si la guarnicion es amarilla, no se estacione ahí. No significa "reservado" ni "solo vehículos oficiales" -- es una prohibicion total de estacionamiento para todos. Busque guarniciones amarillas especialmente cerca de intersecciones, hidrantes y paradas de autobus.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICAN DOS LÍNEAS CONTINUAS AL CENTRO DE UNA VÍA DE CIRCULACIÓN?:
question_translated: What do two continuous (solid) center lines on a road indicate?
question_official_en: What do two continuous lines at the center of a road indicate?

options.text_original: Permite rebasar con precaución
options.text_translated: Passing is allowed with caution
options.text_official_en: Allows passing with caution

options.text_original: Zona peatonal
options.text_translated: Pedestrian zone
options.text_official_en: Pedestrian zone

options.text_original: Que por cada sentido de circulación hay más de un carril y se prohíbe rebasar
options.text_translated: There is more than one lane in each direction, and passing is prohibited
options.text_official_en: That for each direction of travel there is more than one lane and passing is prohibited

explanation_en: Two solid (continuous) center lines mean the road has multiple lanes per direction AND passing is prohibited in both directions. No vehicle may cross the double solid lines to overtake another. This is similar to double yellow lines in the US -- they are a hard no-pass zone. A single dashed line would allow passing.
explanation_es: Dos lineas continuas (solidas) al centro significan que la via tiene multiples carriles por sentido Y se prohibe rebasar en ambas direcciones. Ningún vehículo puedes cruzar las lineas dobles continuas para adelantar a otro. Esto es similar a las lineas dobles amarillas en EE.UU. -- son una zona de no rebasar. Una linea discontinua permitiria rebasar.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - asymmetric narrowing]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Puente
options.text_translated: Bridge
options.text_official_en: Bridge

options.text_original: Estrechamiento asimétrico
options.text_translated: Asymmetric narrowing (road narrows on one side)
options.text_official_en: Asymmetric narrowing

options.text_original: Incorporación al tránsito
options.text_translated: Traffic merge
options.text_official_en: Traffic incorporation

explanation_en: This sign shows an asymmetric narrowing, meaning the road narrows on one side only (not equally from both sides). It is a yellow warning sign that tells you the road ahead will get narrower on one side, so you may need to adjust your lane position. This differs from a symmetric narrowing where both sides narrow equally.
explanation_es: Esta señal indica un estrechamiento asimétrico, lo que significa que el camino se angosta de un solo lado (no igual de ambos lados). Es una señal preventiva amarilla que te indica que el camino adelante se hará más angosto de un lado, por lo que puedes necesitar ajustar tu posición en el carril. Esto difiere de un estrechamiento simétrico donde ambos lados se angostan por igual.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - agricultural machinery prohibited]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Prohibido circular maquinaria agrícola
options.text_translated: Agricultural machinery prohibited
options.text_official_en: Prohibited to drive agricultural machinery

options.text_original: Se autoriza circular con tractor
options.text_translated: Tractor driving authorized
options.text_official_en: Authorized to drive with tractor

options.text_original: Puede circular a baja velocidad
options.text_translated: You may drive at low speed
options.text_official_en: You may drive at low speed

explanation_en: This is a restrictive (regulatory) sign that prohibits agricultural machinery (tractors, combines, etc.) from using this road. Restrictive signs show prohibitions and are typically red/white circles with a diagonal bar. If you see a tractor symbol with a red circle and slash, farm equipment cannot drive on that road.
explanation_es: Está es una señal restrictiva (reglamentaria) que prohibe la circulación de maquinaria agricola (tractores, cosechadoras, etc.) en esta via. Las señales restrictivas muestran prohibiciones y tipicamente son circulos rojos/blancos con una barra diagonal. Si ve un simbolo de tractor con un circulo rojo y diagonal, el equipo agricola no puedes circular en esa via.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - curve ahead]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Vuelta continua
options.text_translated: Continuous right turn
options.text_official_en: Continuous turn

options.text_original: Curva
options.text_translated: Curve ahead
options.text_official_en: Curve

options.text_original: Curva inversa
options.text_translated: Reverse curve (S-curve)
options.text_official_en: Reverse curve

explanation_en: This yellow warning sign indicates a curve ahead. It shows a single curve in the road, alerting drivers to reduce speed. This differs from a 'reverse curve' (curva inversa) which shows an S-shaped path, and from a 'continuous turn' which is a regulatory sign allowing a free right turn at a red light.
explanation_es: Esta señal preventiva amarilla indica una curva adelante. Muestra una sola curva en el camino, alertando a los conductores a reducir la velocidad. Esto difiere de una 'curva inversa' que muestra un camino en forma de S, y de una 'vuelta continua' que es una señal reglamentaria que permite dar vuelta libre a la derecha con semáforo en rojo.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: CUANDO USTED LLEGA A UN CRUCERO Y SE ENCUENTRA CON UNA SEÑAL DE “ALTO” MARCADA EN EL PAVIMENTO, DEBERÁ DETENER SU VEHÍCULO:
question_translated: When you reach an intersection with an 'ALTO' (STOP) marking painted on the pavement, where should you stop your vehicle?
question_official_en: When you arrive at a crossing and find a 'STOP' sign marked on the pavement, you must stop your vehicle:

options.text_original: Sobre la señal de “alto”
options.text_translated: On top of the STOP marking
options.text_official_en: On the 'stop' sign

options.text_original: Antes de la línea blanca marcada para “alto”
options.text_translated: Before the white stop line
options.text_official_en: Before the white line marked for 'stop'

options.text_original: En un lugar cerca de la intersección siempre que haga alto total
options.text_translated: Somewhere near the intersection, as long as you fully stop
options.text_official_en: At a place near the intersection provided you make a complete stop

explanation_en: You must stop BEFORE the white stop line, not on top of the ALTO marking or just anywhere near the intersection. Stopping before the line keeps your vehicle out of the crosswalk and intersection, giving you a clear view of cross traffic and protecting pedestrians. This is a strict rule -- your bumper should not cross the white line.
explanation_es: Debes detenerte ANTES de la línea blanca de alto, no sobre la marca de ALTO ni en cualquier lugar cercano a la intersección. Detenerte antes de la línea mantiene tu vehículo fuera del cruce peatonal y la intersección, dándote una vista clara del tráfico transversal y protegiendo a los peatones. Esta es una regla estricta -- tu defensa no debe cruzar la línea blanca.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICAN UNA LÍNEA CONTINUA Y OTRA DISCONTINUA JUNTAS AL CENTRO DE UNA ARTERIA DE CIRCULACIÓN?:
question_translated: What do a solid line and a dashed line together at the center of a road indicate?
question_official_en: What do a continuous line and a discontinuous line together at the center of a traffic artery indicate?

options.text_original: A quien circula por la línea continua se le prohíbe rebasar y a quien circula por la línea discontinua se le permite rebasar
options.text_translated: Drivers on the solid-line side are prohibited from passing; drivers on the dashed-line side are allowed to pass
options.text_official_en: Those driving on the continuous line side are prohibited from passing, and those driving on the discontinuous line side are allowed to pass

options.text_original: Indica a los conductores el lugar donde deben estacionarse
options.text_translated: It shows drivers where to park
options.text_official_en: It indicates to drivers the place where they should park

options.text_original: Son áreas neutrales
options.text_translated: They are neutral areas
options.text_official_en: They are neutral areas

explanation_en: When you see one solid line and one dashed line together at the road center, the rule depends on which side you are on. If the solid line is on YOUR side, you CANNOT pass. If the dashed line is on your side, you CAN pass when safe. Think of it as: dashed = permission to cross, solid = barrier you cannot cross.
explanation_es: Cuando ve una linea continua y una discontinua juntas al centro del camino, la regla depende del lado en que tú se encuentre. Si la linea continua esta de SU lado, NO PUEDE rebasar. Si la linea discontinua esta de su lado, PUEDE rebasar cuando sea seguro. Pienselo así: discontinua = permiso para cruzar, continua = barrera que no puedes cruzar.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - restricted clearance height]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Peso restringido
options.text_translated: Weight restriction
options.text_official_en: Restricted weight

options.text_original: Altura libre restringida
options.text_translated: Restricted clearance height (low overhead)
options.text_official_en: Restricted clearance height

options.text_original: Estrechamiento simétrico
options.text_translated: Symmetric narrowing (road narrows on both sides)
options.text_official_en: Symmetric narrowing

explanation_en: This sign warns of a restricted clearance height ahead -- meaning there is a bridge, overpass, or tunnel with limited vertical space. Tall vehicles (trucks, buses, RVs) must check their height against the posted limit. This is a restrictive sign showing the maximum height a vehicle can be to safely pass under the structure.
explanation_es: Esta señal advierte sobre una altura libre restringida adelante -- lo que significa que hay un puente, paso a desnivel o tunel con espacio vertical limitado. Los vehículos altos (camiones, autobuses, casas rodantes) deben verificar su altura contra el límite publicado. Está es una señal restrictiva que muestra la altura máxima que un vehículo puedes tener para pasar de manera segura bajo la estructura.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - continuous right turn allowed]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Vuelta continua a la derecha
options.text_translated: Continuous right turn allowed (free right turn on red)
options.text_official_en: Continuous right turn

options.text_original: Codo inverso
options.text_translated: Reverse elbow curve
options.text_official_en: Reverse elbow

options.text_original: Entronque lateral derecho
options.text_translated: Right-side road junction
options.text_official_en: Right lateral junction

explanation_en: This sign indicates a continuous right turn (vuelta continua a la derecha), which means you are permitted to turn right even when the traffic light is red, after yielding to pedestrians and cross traffic. This is similar to the 'right on red' concept in the US, but in Mexico it must be explicitly posted with this sign -- it is not automatic at every intersection.
explanation_es: Esta señal indica una vuelta continua a la derecha, lo que significa que se te permite dar vuelta a la derecha incluso cuando el semáforo está en rojo, después de ceder el paso a peatones y tráfico transversal. Esto es similar al concepto de 'vuelta a la derecha con rojo' en EE.UU., pero en México debe estar explícitamente señalizado con esta señal -- no es automático en cada intersección.

vocabulary.en: continuous right turn / free right on red

source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - narrow bridge]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Desviación
options.text_translated: Detour
options.text_official_en: Detour

options.text_original: Puente angosto
options.text_translated: Narrow bridge ahead
options.text_official_en: Narrow bridge

options.text_original: Circulación al centro
options.text_translated: Drive toward the center
options.text_official_en: Traffic to center

explanation_en: This yellow warning sign indicates a narrow bridge ahead. It warns drivers that the bridge ahead is narrower than the road, so you may need to slow down and be prepared for oncoming traffic in a tighter space. Only one vehicle at a time may be able to cross if the bridge is very narrow.
explanation_es: Esta señal preventiva amarilla indica un puente angosto adelante. Advierte a los conductores que el puente es más angosto que el camino, por lo que puedes necesitar reducir la velocidad y estar preparado para el tráfico en sentido contrario en un espacio más estrecho. Solo un vehículo a la vez podria cruzar si el puente es muy angosto.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ NOS INDICAN LAS LÍNEAS CONTINUAS A LA ORILLA DEL CAMINO?:
question_translated: What do continuous (solid) lines at the edge of the road indicate?
question_official_en: What do the continuous lines at the edge of the road indicate?

options.text_original: Delimitan el espacio para el cruce de peatones
options.text_translated: They mark the pedestrian crossing area
options.text_official_en: They delimit the space for pedestrian crossings

options.text_original: Sirven para separar carriles
options.text_translated: They separate traffic lanes
options.text_official_en: They serve to separate lanes

options.text_original: Delimitan el espacio del acotamiento para circular con seguridad
options.text_translated: They mark the road shoulder boundary so you can drive safely within the road
options.text_official_en: They delimit the shoulder space for safe driving

explanation_en: Continuous (solid) lines at the road edge mark the boundary between the driving surface and the shoulder. They help you stay safely within the road, especially at night, in fog, or on curves. These edge lines are NOT lane separators or pedestrian crossings -- they simply tell you where the road ends and the shoulder begins.
explanation_es: Las líneas continuas (sólidas) a la orilla del camino marcan el límite entre la superficie de conducción y el acotamiento. Te ayudan a mantenerte de manera segura dentro del camino, especialmente de noche, con niebla o en curvas. Estas líneas de borde NO son separadores de carril ni cruces peatonales -- simplemente te indican dónde termina el camino y comienza el acotamiento.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁL DISPOSITIVO MECANICO TIENE LA FUNCIÓN DE ENVIAR CARGA A LA BATERIA?
question_translated: Which mechanical device charges the battery?
question_official_en: Which mechanical device has the function of sending charge to the battery?

options.text_original: El alternador
options.text_translated: The alternator
options.text_official_en: The alternator

options.text_original: Los faros delanteros
options.text_translated: The headlights
options.text_official_en: The front headlights

options.text_original: La bobina
options.text_translated: The ignition coil
options.text_official_en: The coil

explanation_en: The alternator converts mechanical energy from the engine into electrical energy to charge the battery and power the vehicle's electrical system while driving. The headlights consume electricity (they do not produce it), and the ignition coil transforms battery voltage for the spark plugs but does not charge the battery.
explanation_es: El alternador convierte energía mecánica del motor en energía eléctrica para cargar la bateria y alimentar el sistema eléctrico del vehículo mientras se conduce. Los faros consumen electricidad (no la producen), y la bobina transforma el voltaje de la bateria para las bujias pero no carga la bateria.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ CAUSA PUEDE OCASIONAR QUE SU MOTOR SUFRA SOBRECALENTAMIENTO?:
question_translated: What can cause your engine to overheat?
question_official_en: What cause can make your engine suffer overheating?

options.text_original: Falta de agua y banda floja
options.text_translated: Low coolant (water) and a loose belt
options.text_official_en: Lack of water and loose belt

options.text_original: Odómetro en mal estado
options.text_translated: A faulty odometer
options.text_official_en: Odometer in bad condition

options.text_original: Falta de aceite en el diferencial
options.text_translated: Low differential oil
options.text_official_en: Lack of oil in the differential

explanation_en: Engine overheating is caused by low coolant (water) in the radiator and/or a loose drive belt. The belt drives the water pump that circulates coolant through the engine. Without enough coolant or a properly tensioned belt, the engine cannot cool itself. A faulty odometer only measures distance and has nothing to do with cooling. Differential oil lubricates the rear axle gears, not the engine cooling system.
explanation_es: El sobrecalentamiento del motor es causado por falta de agua (refrigerante) en el radiador y/o una banda floja. La banda impulsa la bomba de agua que circula el refrigerante a traves del motor. Sin suficiente refrigerante o una banda con la tension adecuada, el motor no puedes enfriarse. Un odometro defectuoso solo mide distancia y no tiene nada que ver con el enfriamiento. El aceite del diferencial lubrica los engranes del eje trasero, no el sistema de enfriamiento del motor.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - no parking]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Prohibido estacionarse
options.text_translated: No parking
options.text_official_en: Parking prohibited

options.text_original: Zona de estacionamiento
options.text_translated: Parking zone
options.text_official_en: Parking zone

options.text_original: Prohibido seguir de frente
options.text_translated: No going straight ahead
options.text_official_en: Prohibited to continue straight

explanation_en: This is the no-parking sign (prohibido estacionarse). It is a restrictive sign, typically shown as a red circle with a letter 'E' crossed out or with a diagonal bar. It means you cannot park your vehicle in this area at any time. This is different from a 'no stopping' sign, which prohibits even brief stops.
explanation_es: Esta es la señal de prohibido estacionarse. Es una señal restrictiva, tipicamente mostrada como un circulo rojo con la letra 'E' tachada o con una barra diagonal. Significa que no puedes estacionar tu vehículo en esta área en ningún momento. Esto es diferente de la señal de 'no parar', que prohibe incluso paradas breves.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - restricted width]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Estrechamiento asimétrico
options.text_translated: Asymmetric narrowing
options.text_official_en: Asymmetric narrowing

options.text_original: Circulación al centro
options.text_translated: Drive toward the center
options.text_official_en: Traffic to center

options.text_original: Anchura restringida
options.text_translated: Restricted width
options.text_official_en: Restricted width

explanation_en: This sign indicates a restricted width ahead -- meaning the road or passage is narrower than normal and has a specific maximum width limit. Wide vehicles must check their width against the posted limit. This is a restrictive sign, different from the warning sign for 'asymmetric narrowing' which warns you that the road narrows on one side.
explanation_es: Esta señal indica anchura restringida adelante -- lo que significa que el camino o paso es más angosto de lo normal y tiene un límite específico de anchura máxima. Los vehículos anchos deben verificar su anchura contra el límite publicado. Esta es una señal restrictiva, diferente de la señal preventiva de 'estrechamiento asimétrico' que te advierte que el camino se angosta de un lado.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁLES SON LAS HERRAMIENTAS BÁSICAS QUE DEBE TRAER EN SU VEHÍCULO?
question_translated: What are the basic tools you should carry in your vehicle?
question_official_en: What are the basic tools that you should carry in your vehicle?

options.text_original: Tabla, Martillo, Clavos, Pijas, Lijas, Navaja, Pegamento líquido
options.text_translated: Board, hammer, nails, screws, sandpaper, knife, liquid glue
options.text_official_en: Board, hammer, nails, screws, sandpaper, knife, liquid adhesive

options.text_original: Trapos, Focos, Bandas, Mangueras, Cinta aislante, Bobina y Liquido para frenos
options.text_translated: Rags, light bulbs, belts, hoses, electrical tape, ignition coil, and brake fluid
options.text_official_en: Rags, bulbs, belts, hoses, insulating tape, coil, and brake fluid

options.text_original: Cables para pasar corriente, Lámpara, Gato, Llave de cruz, Pinzas, Franela, Desarmadores de cruz y planos. Manómetro para llantas
options.text_translated: Jumper cables, flashlight, jack, lug wrench, pliers, cloth, Phillips and flathead screwdrivers, and a tire pressure gauge
options.text_official_en: Cables for jumping current, lamp, jack, cross wrench, pliers, flannel, Phillips and flat screwdrivers, tire pressure gauge

explanation_en: The basic tools every vehicle should carry are: jumper cables (for dead batteries), a flashlight, a jack and lug wrench (for changing tires), pliers, a cloth/rag, Phillips and flathead screwdrivers, and a tire pressure gauge. Option A lists woodworking tools, and Option B lists spare parts -- neither are the standard emergency toolkit for a vehicle.
explanation_es: Las herramientas basicas que todo vehículo debes traer son: cables para pasar corriente (para baterias descargadas), una lampara, un gato y llave de cruz (para cambiar llantas), pinzas, una franela, desarmadores de cruz y planos, y un manometro para llantas. La opcion A lista herramientas de carpinteria, y la opcion B lista refacciones -- ninguna es el kit de emergencia estándar para un vehículo.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - no stopping]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: No parar
options.text_translated: No stopping (do not stop at all, not even briefly)
options.text_official_en: No stopping

options.text_original: Cruce de ferrocarril
options.text_translated: Railroad crossing
options.text_official_en: Railroad crossing

options.text_original: Cruce de caminos
options.text_translated: Road crossing / Crossroads
options.text_official_en: Road crossing

explanation_en: This is the no-stopping sign (no parar), which is stricter than the no-parking sign. It means you cannot stop your vehicle at all -- not even briefly to pick up or drop off passengers. This sign is typically a restrictive sign with an 'X' pattern. Do not confuse it with 'no parking,' which allows brief stops.
explanation_es: Esta es la señal de no parar, que es más estricta que la señal de no estacionarse. Significa que no puedes detener tu vehículo en absoluto -- ni siquiera brevemente para subir o bajar pasajeros. Esta señal es tipicamente una señal restrictiva con un patron en 'X'. No la confunda con 'prohibido estacionarse', que permite paradas breves.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - crossroads]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Cruce de ferrocarril
options.text_translated: Railroad crossing
options.text_official_en: Railroad crossing

options.text_original: Cruce de caminos
options.text_translated: Road crossing / Crossroads
options.text_official_en: Road crossing

options.text_original: No parar
options.text_translated: No stopping
options.text_official_en: No stopping

explanation_en: This warning sign indicates a crossroads (cruce de caminos) -- an intersection where another road crosses your path. It is a yellow preventive sign that warns you to be alert for vehicles entering from side roads. This is different from a railroad crossing sign, which specifically warns about train tracks.
explanation_es: Esta señal preventiva indica un cruce de caminos -- una intersección donde otro camino cruza tu trayectoria. Es una señal preventiva amarilla que te advierte estar alerta ante vehículos que entran desde caminos laterales. Esto es diferente de la señal de cruce de ferrocarril, que advierte específicamente sobre vías de tren.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - U-turn prohibited]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Glorieta
options.text_translated: Roundabout
options.text_official_en: Roundabout

options.text_original: Prohibida la vuelta
options.text_translated: Turn prohibited
options.text_official_en: Turn prohibited

options.text_original: Prohibido el retorno
options.text_translated: U-turn prohibited
options.text_official_en: U-turn prohibited

explanation_en: This restrictive sign prohibits U-turns (retornos). It is different from a 'turn prohibited' sign, which blocks left or right turns, and different from a roundabout sign, which indicates a traffic circle ahead. The U-turn prohibition means you cannot reverse your direction of travel at this location -- you must find an alternative route.
explanation_es: Esta señal restrictiva prohibe los retornos (vuelta en U). Es diferente de una señal de 'prohibida la vuelta', que bloquea vueltas a la izquierda o derecha, y diferente de una señal de glorieta, que indica una rotonda adelante. La prohibicion de retorno significa que no puedes invertir su dirección de viaje en este lugar -- debes buscar una ruta alternativa.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate? [IMAGE: road sign - yield / you must yield the right of way]
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Le ceden a usted el paso
options.text_translated: Others yield to you
options.text_official_en: They yield the right of way to you

options.text_original: Usted debe ceder el paso
options.text_translated: You must yield the right of way
options.text_official_en: You must yield the right of way

options.text_original: Los dos tienen el paso
options.text_translated: Both have the right of way
options.text_official_en: Both have the right of way

explanation_en: This is the yield sign (ceda el paso), typically an inverted triangle. It means YOU must yield -- let other traffic go first before proceeding. It does NOT mean others yield to you. When you see this sign, slow down and be prepared to stop if needed to give way to cross traffic or merging vehicles.
explanation_es: Esta es la señal de ceda el paso, típicamente un triángulo invertido. Significa que tú debes ceder -- dejar pasar al otro tráfico primero antes de continuar. NO significa que otros te ceden el paso. Cuando veas esta señal, reduce la velocidad y estate preparado para detenerte si es necesario para dar paso al tráfico transversal o vehículos que se incorporan.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate?
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Salida
options.text_translated: Exit
options.text_official_en: Exit

options.text_original: Entronque lateral derecho
options.text_translated: Right lateral junction
options.text_official_en: Right lateral junction

options.text_original: Incorporación de tránsito
options.text_translated: Traffic merging
options.text_official_en: Traffic incorporation

explanation_en: This sign indicates an exit (salida) from a highway or major road. It warns drivers that a ramp or lane is available to leave the main roadway. Recognizing exit signs is essential for navigation and safe lane changes.
explanation_es: Esta señal indica una salida de una autopista o carretera principal. Advierte a los conductores que hay una rampa o carril disponible para abandonar la via principal. Reconocer las señales de salida es esencial para la navegacion y los cambios de carril seguros.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ INDICA ESTA SEÑAL?:
question_translated: What does this sign indicate?
question_official_en: What does this sign indicate?

options.text_original: Un solo carril
options.text_translated: Single lane
options.text_official_en: A single lane

options.text_original: Circular al centro
options.text_translated: Drive in center
options.text_official_en: Circulate in the center

options.text_original: Anchura libre
options.text_translated: Free width (clearance)
options.text_official_en: Free width

explanation_en: This sign indicates the free width (anchura libre) -- the available clearance width of a road, bridge, or tunnel. It tells drivers the maximum width a vehicle can be to pass through safely. This is critical for trucks and wide vehicles.
explanation_es: Esta señal indica la anchura libre -- el ancho de espacio disponible en una carretera, puente o túnel. Les dice a los conductores el ancho máximo que puede tener un vehículo para pasar de manera segura. Esto es crítico para camiones y vehículos anchos.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ MEDIDAS DE SEGURIDAD DEBE TOMAR SI SU VEHÍCULO SUFRE ALGUNA FALLA MECÁNICA DURANTE SU RECORRIDO?
question_translated: What safety measures should you take if your vehicle has a mechanical failure while driving?
question_official_en: What safety measures should you take if your vehicle suffers a mechanical failure during your route?

options.text_original: Encender luces de emergencia, buscar cuál es la falla y repararla rápidamente en el lugar
options.text_translated: Turn on hazard lights, find the problem, and repair it quickly on the spot
options.text_official_en: Turn on emergency lights, look for the failure and repair it quickly in place

options.text_original: Encender luces de emergencia, mover el vehículo a la extrema derecha de manera que no obstruya la circulación y colocar las señales preventivas correspondientes
options.text_translated: Turn on hazard lights, move the vehicle to the far right so it does not block traffic, and set up warning signs
options.text_official_en: Turn on emergency lights, move the vehicle to the far right so that it does not obstruct circulation, and place the corresponding warning signs

options.text_original: Encender luces de emergencia y esperar en el lugar a que llegue la ayuda del mecánico
options.text_translated: Turn on hazard lights and wait where you are for a mechanic to arrive
options.text_official_en: Turn on emergency lights and wait in place for the mechanic's help to arrive

explanation_en: When your vehicle breaks down on the road, the priority is to make yourself visible and get out of the flow of traffic. Turn on your hazard lights immediately, then carefully move to the far right shoulder so you do not block other vehicles. Finally, place warning triangles or flares behind your vehicle to alert approaching drivers.
explanation_es: Cuando tu vehículo se descompone en la carretera, la prioridad es hacerse visible y salir del flujo de tráfico. Encienda las luces de emergencia de inmediato, luego muevase con cuidado al extremo derecho para no bloquear a otros vehículos. Finalmente, coloque triangulos de advertencia o señales preventivas detrás de tu vehículo para alertar a los conductores que se aproximan.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: LOS ESPEJOS RETROVISORES DEBEN OBSERVARSE CADA:
question_translated: How often should you check your rearview mirrors?
question_official_en: The rearview mirrors should be observed every:

options.text_original: 1 a 2 minutos
options.text_translated: Every 1 to 2 minutes
options.text_official_en: 1 to 2 minutes

options.text_original: 2 a 5 segundos
options.text_translated: Every 2 to 5 seconds
options.text_official_en: 2 to 5 seconds

options.text_original: 30 a 40 segundos
options.text_translated: Every 30 to 40 seconds
options.text_official_en: 30 to 40 seconds

explanation_en: You should glance at your rearview mirrors every 2 to 5 seconds. This keeps you constantly aware of vehicles behind and beside you, which is essential for safe lane changes, braking, and reacting to emergencies. Checking less frequently creates dangerous blind spots in your awareness.
explanation_es: Debes revisar tus espejos retrovisores cada 2 a 5 segundos. Esto te mantiene constantemente al tanto de los vehículos detrás y a tu lado, lo cual es esencial para cambios de carril seguros, frenado y reacción ante emergencias. Revisarlos con menos frecuencia crea puntos ciegos peligrosos en tu percepción.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ANTES DE INICIAR LA CONDUCCIÓN DE SU VEHÍCULO ¿QUE PUNTOS BÁSICOS DE SEGURIDAD ES CONVENIENTE REVISAR?
question_translated: Before driving your vehicle, what basic safety items should you check?
question_official_en: Before starting to drive your vehicle, what basic safety points is it advisable to check?

options.text_original: Llantas, luces, tarjeta de circulación y licencia
options.text_translated: Tires, lights, registration card, and license
options.text_official_en: Tires, lights, circulation card and license

options.text_original: Amortiguadores, extinguidor y refacción
options.text_translated: Shock absorbers, fire extinguisher, and spare tire
options.text_official_en: Shock absorbers, extinguisher and spare

options.text_original: Aceite, agua, llantas, luces, espejos y combustible
options.text_translated: Oil, water, tires, lights, mirrors, and fuel
options.text_official_en: Oil, water, tires, lights, mirrors and fuel

explanation_en: Before every drive, you should check six key items: oil level, coolant/water, tire condition and pressure, all lights, mirror adjustment, and fuel level. This comprehensive pre-drive check ensures your vehicle is mechanically safe and reduces the risk of breakdowns or accidents caused by preventable failures.
explanation_es: Antes de cada viaje, debes revisar seis elementos clave: nivel de aceite, refrigerante/agua, estado y presion de llantas, todas las luces, ajuste de espejos y nivel de combustible. Esta revisión previa integral asegura que tu vehículo este mecanicamente seguro y reduce el riesgo de descomposturas o accidentes causados por fallas prevenibles.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: CUANDO UN VEHÍCULO LO ESTÁ REBASANDO, ¿QUÉ DEBE HACER?:
question_translated: When another vehicle is passing you, what should you do?
question_official_en: When a vehicle is overtaking you, what should you do?

options.text_original: Aumentar la velocidad
options.text_translated: Speed up
options.text_official_en: Increase speed

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad
options.text_translated: Slow down
options.text_official_en: Decrease speed

options.text_original: Activar intermitentes
options.text_translated: Turn on your turn signals
options.text_official_en: Activate turn signals

explanation_en: When another vehicle is passing you, you should slow down to make the maneuver safer and faster. Speeding up while being overtaken forces the passing vehicle to stay in the oncoming lane longer, which dramatically increases the risk of a head-on collision.
explanation_es: Cuando otro vehículo lo esta rebasando, debes disminuir la velocidad para hacer la maniobra más segura y rápida. Acelerar mientras lo rebasan obliga al vehículo que pasa a permanecer más tiempo en el carril contrario, lo que aumenta dramaticamente el riesgo de una colisión frontal.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SI AL CONDUCIR SU VEHÍCULO UNA DE LAS LLANTAS REVIENTA, INMEDIATAMENTE USTED DEBE DE:
question_translated: If one of your tires blows out while driving, what should you do immediately?
question_official_en: If while driving your vehicle one of the tires blows out, you should immediately:

options.text_original: Aplicar el pedal de los frenos para reducir la velocidad
options.text_translated: Hit the brakes to slow down
options.text_official_en: Apply the brake pedal to reduce speed

options.text_original: Sujetar con fuerza el volante, quitar el pie del acelerador y dejar que el vehículo vaya perdiendo velocidad antes que lo dirija al borde derecho del camino
options.text_translated: Grip the steering wheel firmly, take your foot off the gas, let the car slow down on its own, then steer to the right side of the road
options.text_official_en: Firmly grip the steering wheel, remove your foot from the accelerator and let the vehicle lose speed before directing it to the right edge of the road

options.text_original: Rápidamente dirigir el vehículo al lado derecho del camino
options.text_translated: Immediately steer to the right side of the road
options.text_official_en: Quickly direct the vehicle to the right side of the road

explanation_en: During a tire blowout, slamming the brakes or jerking the steering wheel can cause you to lose control. The correct response is to grip the wheel firmly to maintain control, release the accelerator to let the car slow down gradually, and only then gently steer toward the right shoulder once the vehicle is at a manageable speed.
explanation_es: Durante un reventon de llanta, frenar bruscamente o girar el volante de golpe puedes hacerle perder el control. La respuesta correcta es sujetar el volante con firmeza para mantener el control, soltar el acelerador para que el auto desacelere gradualmente, y solo entonces dirigirse suavemente hacia el acotamiento derecho una vez que el vehículo este a una velocidad manejable.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: EL CHOQUE MÁS PELIGROSO EN UN ACCIDENTE, CONSIDERANDO VELOCIDADES SIMILARES ES:
question_translated: What is the most dangerous type of collision at similar speeds?
question_official_en: The most dangerous collision in an accident, considering similar speeds, is:

options.text_original: El de costado
options.text_translated: Side collision
options.text_official_en: The side collision

options.text_original: Por alcance
options.text_translated: Rear-end collision
options.text_official_en: Rear-end collision

options.text_original: El de frente
options.text_translated: Head-on collision
options.text_official_en: The head-on collision

explanation_en: A head-on collision is the most dangerous type because the speeds of both vehicles combine on impact. If two cars traveling at 60 km/h collide head-on, the impact force is equivalent to hitting a wall at 120 km/h. In rear-end collisions at similar speeds, the speed difference is much smaller, resulting in less force.
explanation_es: Una colisión frontal es el tipo más peligroso porque las velocidades de ambos vehículos se combinan en el impacto. Si dos autos que viajan a 60 km/h chocan de frente, la fuerza del impacto es equivalente a chocar contra un muro a 120 km/h. En colisiones por alcance a velocidades similares, la diferencia de velocidad es mucho menor, resultando en menos fuerza.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ DEBE HACER SI AL CONDUCIR SE LEVANTA LA TAPA DEL COFRE DE SU VEHÍCULO?:
question_translated: What should you do if the hood of your car flies up while driving?
question_official_en: What should you do if while driving, the hood cover of your vehicle lifts up?

options.text_original: Frenar rápidamente
options.text_translated: Brake hard immediately
options.text_official_en: Brake quickly

options.text_original: Dirigirse directamente a la derecha
options.text_translated: Steer directly to the right
options.text_official_en: Direct yourself immediately to the right

options.text_original: Sacar la cabeza por la ventanilla, bajar la velocidad y orillarse a la derecha
options.text_translated: Look out your side window, slow down, and pull over to the right
options.text_official_en: Stick your head out the window, reduce speed and pull over to the right

explanation_en: If your hood flies up and blocks your windshield, do NOT slam the brakes or swerve blindly. Instead, look out the side window to see the road, gradually slow down, and carefully pull over to the right shoulder. This controlled approach prevents panic-driven accidents while you safely get off the road.
explanation_es: Si el cofre se levanta y bloquea tu parabrisas, NO frenes bruscamente ni gires a ciegas. En su lugar, mira por la ventanilla lateral para ver el camino, disminuye la velocidad gradualmente y oríllate con cuidado hacia el acotamiento derecho. Este enfoque controlado previene accidentes por pánico mientras sales de la carretera de manera segura.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ PRECAUCIONES DEBE USTED TOMAR PARA NO INVADIR LA ZONA PEATONAL?
question_translated: What precautions should you take to avoid entering the pedestrian zone?
question_official_en: What precautions should you take to not invade the pedestrian zone?

options.text_original: Acelerar para cruzar rápidamente la zona
options.text_translated: Speed up to cross the zone quickly
options.text_official_en: Accelerate to quickly cross the zone

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad si el semáforo cambió a preventiva y frenar antes de la línea blanca
options.text_translated: Slow down when the light turns yellow and brake before the white line
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed if the traffic light changed to caution and brake before the white line

options.text_original: Frenar después de haber pasado la zona peatonal
options.text_translated: Brake after you have already passed the pedestrian zone
options.text_official_en: Brake after having passed the pedestrian zone

explanation_en: To protect pedestrians, you must slow down when you see the traffic light turn yellow and come to a complete stop before the white crosswalk line. Stopping beyond the white line puts your vehicle in the pedestrian crossing area, endangering people on foot. The yellow light is your warning to prepare to stop, not to speed through.
explanation_es: Para proteger a los peatones, debes disminuir la velocidad cuando vea que el semáforo cambia a amarillo y detenerse completamente antes de la linea blanca del cruce peatonal. Detenerse más alla de la linea blanca pone tu vehículo en el área de cruce peatonal, poniendo en peligro a las personas a pie. La luz amarilla es su advertencia para prepararse a detenerse, no para acelerar.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ES FRECUENTE AL CIRCULAR EN LAS CALLES, ENCONTRARSE ALGÚN CONDUCTOR QUE NOS SIGUE DEMASIADO CERCA, PRESIONÁNDONOS YA SEA CON LUCES O CLAXON. ¿CUÁL DEBE DE SER NUESTRA ACTITUD ANTE ESTA SITUACIÓN?
question_translated: When another driver is tailgating you aggressively with lights or horn, what should you do?
question_official_en: It is common when driving on the streets to encounter a driver who follows us too closely, pressuring us with lights or horn. What should be our attitude in this situation?

options.text_original: Acelerar para evitar que pase
options.text_translated: Speed up to prevent them from passing
options.text_official_en: Accelerate to prevent them from passing

options.text_original: Cambiar cuidadosamente de carril, previo encendido de las direccionales correspondientes
options.text_translated: Carefully change lanes after turning on your turn signal
options.text_official_en: Carefully change lanes, with prior activation of the corresponding turn signals

options.text_original: Continuar manejando por mi carril y dejar que el otro conductor me rebase
options.text_translated: Stay in your lane and let the other driver pass you
options.text_official_en: Continue driving in my lane and let the other driver overtake me

explanation_en: When an aggressive driver is tailgating you, the safest response is to signal and carefully change lanes to let them pass. Speeding up escalates the situation, and simply staying put keeps you in danger from a rear-end collision. By actively and safely moving out of their way, you de-escalate the situation and protect yourself.
explanation_es: Cuando un conductor agresivo lo sigue de cerca, la respuesta más segura es senalizar y cambiar de carril con cuidado para dejarlos pasar. Acelerar escala la situación, y simplemente quedarse en su lugar lo mantiene en peligro de un choque por alcance. Al moverse activa y seguramente fuera de su camino, desescala la situación y se protege.

vocabulary.en: to pressure with lights or horn

source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ DEBEMOS HACER COMO MEDIDA DE SEGURIDAD AL APROXIMARNOS A UN CRUCERO EN EL QUE LAS VÍAS SON DE IGUAL IMPORTANCIA Y NO EXISTE SEMÁFORO NI SEÑAL DE ALTO?
question_translated: What should you do when approaching an intersection where the roads are equally important and there is no traffic light or stop sign?
question_official_en: What should we do as a safety measure when approaching an intersection where the roads are of equal importance and there is no traffic light or stop sign?

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad, quitar el pie del acelerador, posarlo suavemente en el freno, detenerse, ver hacia los lados y avanzar con cuidado
options.text_translated: Slow down, take your foot off the gas, gently rest it on the brake, stop, look both ways, and proceed carefully
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed, remove foot from the accelerator, gently place it on the brake, stop, look to the sides and proceed with caution

options.text_original: Observar hacia los lados y continuar la marcha a la misma velocidad
options.text_translated: Look both ways and keep going at the same speed
options.text_official_en: Observe to the sides and continue at the same speed

options.text_original: Acelerar para que me cedan el paso ya que llegué primero
options.text_translated: Speed up so they yield to you since you arrived first
options.text_official_en: Accelerate so that they yield the right of way since I arrived first

explanation_en: At an uncontrolled intersection where roads have equal priority, you must slow down, stop completely, and look both ways before proceeding cautiously. Without signals or signs, no one has automatic right of way, so assuming you can speed through is extremely dangerous. This methodical approach prevents collisions with vehicles you may not see.
explanation_es: En un cruce sin control donde las vias tienen igual prioridad, debes disminuir la velocidad, detenerse completamente y mirar a ambos lados antes de avanzar con precaución. Sin semáforos ni señales, nadie tiene derecho de paso automático, por lo que asumir que puedes pasar a velocidad es extremadamente peligroso. Este enfoque metodico previene colisiones con vehículos que podria no ver.

vocabulary.en: to yield the right of way

source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ALGUNOS EXPERTOS EN LA SEGURIDAD VIAL CLASIFICAN VARIOS TIPOS DE CONDUCTORES SEGÚN SU ACTITUD PELIGROSA AL CONDUCIR, LOS MÁS COMUNES SON:
question_translated: According to road safety experts, what are the most common types of dangerous drivers?
question_official_en: Some road safety experts classify various types of drivers according to their dangerous driving attitude. The most common are:

options.text_original: Alegres, simpáticos y amables
options.text_translated: Happy, friendly, and kind
options.text_official_en: Happy, friendly and kind

options.text_original: Tranquilos y descuidados
options.text_translated: Calm and careless
options.text_official_en: Calm and careless

options.text_original: Agresivos, Ofensivos, Defensivos
options.text_translated: Aggressive, offensive, and defensive
options.text_official_en: Aggressive, offensive, defensive

explanation_en: Road safety experts identify three dangerous driver personality types: aggressive drivers (who drive with hostility), offensive drivers (who intentionally violate rules), and defensive drivers (who drive overly cautiously, disrupting traffic flow). Note that in this Mexican classification, 'defensive' drivers are considered problematic because excessive hesitation can be as dangerous as aggression.
explanation_es: Los expertos en seguridad vial identifican tres tipos de personalidad peligrosa al volante: conductores agresivos (que manejan con hostilidad), ofensivos (que violan las reglas intencionalmente) y defensivos (que manejan con excesiva precaución, interrumpiendo el flujo del tráfico). En esta clasificación mexicana, los conductores 'defensivos' se consideran problematicos porque la vacilacion excesiva puedes ser tan peligrosa como la agresion.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: CUANDO ESTAMOS CANSADOS Y CONDUCIMOS UN VEHÍCULO, SE RECOMIENDA POR SEGURIDAD:
question_translated: When you are tired and driving, what is recommended for safety?
question_official_en: When we are tired and driving a vehicle, it is recommended for safety:

options.text_original: Tomar dos tazas de café bien cargado sin azúcar
options.text_translated: Drink two cups of strong black coffee
options.text_official_en: Drink two cups of strong coffee without sugar

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad, bajar un poco el cristal del lado del conductor de tal forma que permita entrada de aire y encender el radio a alto volumen
options.text_translated: Slow down, open the driver's window slightly for air, and turn the radio up loud
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed, lower the driver's side window slightly to allow air entry and turn on the radio at high volume

options.text_original: Detenernos a descansar durante al menos 20 minutos y hacer algunos ejercicios físicos antes de continuar nuestro viaje
options.text_translated: Stop and rest for at least 20 minutes and do some physical exercises before continuing
options.text_official_en: Stop to rest for at least 20 minutes and do some physical exercises before continuing our trip

explanation_en: The only effective remedy for driving fatigue is to stop and rest. Coffee, fresh air, and loud music are temporary stimulants that do not address the underlying exhaustion. Stopping for at least 20 minutes and doing light physical exercises restores alertness and significantly reduces your accident risk.
explanation_es: El único remedio efectivo para la fatiga al manejar es detenerse y descansar. El cafe, el aire fresco y la musica fuerte son estimulantes temporales que no abordan el cansancio subyacente. Detenerse al menos 20 minutos y hacer ejercicios físicos ligeros restaura el estado de alerta y reduce significativamente el riesgo de accidente.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁLES SON LAS CONDICIONES POTENCIALMENTE ADVERSAS EN LA CONDUCCIÓN?
question_translated: What are potentially adverse driving conditions?
question_official_en: What are the potentially adverse conditions in driving?

options.text_original: El tiempo, el tráfico, el vehículo, el camino y la iluminación
options.text_translated: Weather, traffic, the vehicle, the road, and lighting
options.text_official_en: The weather, the traffic, the vehicle, the road and the lighting

options.text_original: Conducir con precaución, Respetar las zonas peatonales, conducir en sus cinco sentidos
options.text_translated: Driving cautiously, respecting pedestrian zones, driving with all your senses
options.text_official_en: Driving with caution, respecting pedestrian zones, driving with all five senses

options.text_original: Un conductor responsable, un peatón precavido y un pasajero atento
options.text_translated: A responsible driver, a cautious pedestrian, and an attentive passenger
options.text_official_en: A responsible driver, a cautious pedestrian and an attentive passenger

explanation_en: The five potentially adverse driving conditions are: weather (rain, fog, wind), traffic (volume, congestion), vehicle condition (mechanical issues), road condition (potholes, construction), and lighting (darkness, glare). Recognizing these factors helps you adjust your driving behavior proactively to stay safe.
explanation_es: Las cinco condiciones potencialmente adversas al manejar son: el clima (lluvia, niebla, viento), el tráfico (volumen, congestión), el estado del vehículo (problemas mecánicos), el estado del camino (baches, construcción) y la iluminación (oscuridad, deslumbramiento). Reconocer estos factores te ayuda a ajustar tu conducción proactivamente para mantenerte seguro.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁL ES LA FORMA MÁS SEGURA PARA TOMAR UNA CURVA?:
question_translated: What is the safest way to take a curve?
question_official_en: What is the safest way to take a curve?

options.text_original: Antes de entrar, disminuir la velocidad y acelerar paulatinamente dentro de la curva
options.text_translated: Slow down before entering the curve, then accelerate gradually through it
options.text_official_en: Before entering, reduce speed and accelerate gradually within the curve

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad dentro de la curva
options.text_translated: Slow down while inside the curve
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed within the curve

options.text_original: Tomar con suavidad el volante y continuar con la velocidad normal
options.text_translated: Hold the steering wheel gently and maintain your normal speed
options.text_official_en: Gently hold the steering wheel and continue at normal speed

explanation_en: The safest technique is to reduce your speed BEFORE entering the curve, then gradually accelerate through it. Braking inside a curve reduces tire grip and can cause skidding or loss of control. Gentle acceleration through the curve transfers weight to the rear tires, actually improving traction and stability.
explanation_es: La técnica más segura es reducir la velocidad ANTES de entrar a la curva, y luego acelerar gradualmente a traves de ella. Frenar dentro de una curva reduce la adherencia de las llantas y puedes causar derrapes o perdida de control. La aceleracion suave a traves de la curva transfiere peso a las llantas traseras, mejorando la tracción y estabilidad.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ DEBE USTED HACER SI SE EMPIEZA A INCENDIAR SU VEHÍCULO?:
question_translated: What should you do if your vehicle catches fire?
question_official_en: What should you do if your vehicle starts to catch fire?

options.text_original: Llamar a los bomberos y pedir auxilio a los transeúntes
options.text_translated: Call the fire department and ask bystanders for help
options.text_official_en: Call the firefighters and ask passers-by for help

options.text_original: Abrir las puertas y echarle agua para apagarlo
options.text_translated: Open the doors and throw water on it to put it out
options.text_official_en: Open the doors and throw water to extinguish it

options.text_original: Apagar el fuego con el extinguidor de incendios de su auto
options.text_translated: Put out the fire with your car's fire extinguisher
options.text_official_en: Extinguish the fire with the fire extinguisher in your car

explanation_en: Your first action should be to use your vehicle's fire extinguisher, which is required equipment in Mexico. Vehicle fires can spread rapidly, so immediate action is critical. Waiting for firefighters wastes precious time, and throwing water on an engine fire (which may involve oil or electrical components) can actually make it worse.
explanation_es: Su primera accion debes ser usar el extintor de incendios de tu vehículo, que es equipo obligatorio en Mexico. Los incendios vehiculares pueden propagarse rápidamente, por lo que la accion inmediata es critica. Esperar a los bomberos desperdicia tiempo valioso, y echar agua en un incendio del motor (que puedes involucrar aceite o componentes eléctricos) puedes empeorarlo.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CÓMO DEBEMOS PROCEDER POR SEGURIDAD SI EL PISO POR DONDE VAMOS TRANSITANDO ESTÁ MOJADO?
question_translated: What should you do when driving on a wet road?
question_official_en: How should we proceed for safety if the road surface where we are driving is wet?

options.text_original: Debemos disminuir la velocidad de circulación y darle mayor distancia al vehículo que nos antecede
options.text_translated: Reduce your speed and increase the following distance from the vehicle ahead
options.text_official_en: We should reduce our driving speed and give greater distance to the vehicle ahead of us

options.text_original: Podemos mantener nuestra velocidad normal si están en buen estado los frenos y las llantas
options.text_translated: You can maintain normal speed if your brakes and tires are in good condition
options.text_official_en: We can maintain our normal speed if the brakes and tires are in good condition

options.text_original: Debemos disminuir la velocidad solamente en las inmediaciones de los cruceros
options.text_translated: Only slow down near intersections
options.text_official_en: We should reduce speed only in the vicinity of intersections

explanation_en: On wet roads, you need to do TWO things: reduce your speed and increase your following distance. Water on the road reduces tire grip significantly, meaning your stopping distance increases. Even with good brakes and tires, the road surface itself becomes slippery, so maintaining normal speed is never safe on wet pavement.
explanation_es: En carreteras mojadas, debes hacer DOS cosas: reducir la velocidad y aumentar la distancia de seguimiento. El agua en el camino reduce significativamente la adherencia de las llantas, lo que significa que la distancia de frenado aumenta. Incluso con buenos frenos y llantas, la superficie del camino se vuelve resbalosa, por lo que mantener la velocidad normal nunca es seguro en pavimento mojado.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: HAY PERSONAS QUE CONDUCEN SUS VEHÍCULOS EN FORMA INADECUADA Y PELIGROSA, ¿QUÉ DEBE USTED HACER SI EN SU CAMINO SE ENCUENTRA CON ALGUNO DE ELLOS?
question_translated: What should you do if you encounter a dangerous driver on the road?
question_official_en: There are people who drive their vehicles in an inadequate and dangerous manner. What should you do if on your route you encounter one of them?

options.text_original: Guardar la calma, ceder un poco, alejarse y pensar que su seguridad es lo más importante
options.text_translated: Stay calm, yield a little, distance yourself, and remember that your safety is the most important thing
options.text_official_en: Keep calm, yield a little, move away and think that your safety is the most important thing

options.text_original: Darles alcance para indicarles los errores que van cometiendo al conducir
options.text_translated: Catch up to them to point out the driving mistakes they are making
options.text_official_en: Catch up to them to indicate the errors they are committing while driving
