options.text_original: Ubicarse en el mismo carril a unos 7 metros adelante o atrás y cometer los mismos errores para que el otro conductor aprenda
options.text_translated: Position yourself in the same lane about 7 meters ahead or behind and make the same mistakes so they learn
options.text_official_en: Position yourself in the same lane about 7 meters ahead or behind and commit the same errors so that the other driver learns

explanation_en: When you encounter a reckless driver, your priority is your own safety. Stay calm, give them space, and distance yourself from the danger. Trying to confront or educate them while driving only escalates the situation and puts you at greater risk. Let them pass and focus on protecting yourself and your passengers.
explanation_es: Cuando se encuentra con un conductor imprudente, su prioridad es su propia seguridad. Mantenga la calma, dele espacio y alejese del peligro. Intentar confrontarlos o educarlos mientras conduce solo escala la situación y lo pone en mayor riesgo. Dejelos pasar y enfoquese en protegerse a ti y a tus pasajeros.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ DEBE HACER SI AL LLEGAR A UN CRUCERO EL SEMÁFORO LE MARCA LA LUZ ÁMBAR?
question_translated: What should you do if you reach an intersection and the traffic light turns yellow?
question_official_en: What should you do if upon arriving at an intersection the traffic light shows the amber light?

options.text_original: Frenar rápidamente para no pasarse el alto
options.text_translated: Brake hard to avoid running the red light
options.text_official_en: Brake quickly so as not to run the red light

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad frenando moderadamente para evitar el accidente con el vehículo que le sigue
options.text_translated: Slow down by braking moderately to avoid being rear-ended by the vehicle behind you
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed by braking moderately to avoid an accident with the vehicle that follows you

options.text_original: Sonar tres veces la bocina para que el conductor que le sigue lo escuche
options.text_translated: Honk three times so the driver behind you hears you
options.text_official_en: Honk the horn three times so that the driver behind you hears

explanation_en: When a traffic light turns yellow, you should brake moderately -- not slam on the brakes. Slamming the brakes at a yellow light can cause the vehicle behind you to rear-end you, especially if they were not expecting a sudden stop. Gradual, moderate braking gives everyone time to react safely.
explanation_es: Cuando el semáforo cambia a amarillo, debes frenar moderadamente -- no frenar de golpe. Frenar bruscamente en una luz amarilla puedes causar que el vehículo detrás de ti lo choque por alcance, especialmente si no esperaban una parada subita. El frenado gradual y moderado da tiempo a todos para reaccionar de manera segura.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁL ES EL COMPORTAMIENTO DE UN CONDUCTOR PROFESIONAL ANTE UN CONGESTIONAMIENTO VIAL?
question_translated: How should a professional driver behave in a traffic jam?
question_official_en: What is the behavior of a professional driver in the face of a traffic jam?

options.text_original: Circula entre los vehículos aprovechando los espacios, procurando tener pocos incidentes viales
options.text_translated: Weave between vehicles using open spaces, trying to have few incidents
options.text_official_en: Circulate between vehicles taking advantage of spaces, trying to have few traffic incidents

options.text_original: Toca el claxon insistentemente y cambia de carril intempestivamente
options.text_translated: Honk the horn insistently and change lanes abruptly
options.text_official_en: Honk the horn insistently and change lanes abruptly

options.text_original: Espera su turno indicado por el semáforo, no se desespera y busca vías alternas
options.text_translated: Wait your turn at the traffic light, stay patient, and look for alternate routes
options.text_official_en: Wait your turn indicated by the traffic light, do not get frustrated and look for alternate routes

explanation_en: A professional driver stays patient in traffic jams. They wait their turn at traffic lights, avoid frustration, and calmly seek alternate routes if available. Weaving between vehicles or honking aggressively increases accident risk and road rage. Patience and composure are the marks of a skilled, responsible driver.
explanation_es: Un conductor profesional mantiene la paciencia en los congestionamientos viales. Espera su turno en los semáforos, evita la frustracion y busca rutas alternas con calma si están disponibles. Zigzaguear entre vehículos o tocar el claxon agresivamente aumenta el riesgo de accidentes y la ira al volante. La paciencia y la compostura son las marcas de un conductor hábil y responsable.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁNDO SE CONSIDERA QUE LAS LLANTAS DE LOS VEHÍCULOS NO SON SEGURAS?
question_translated: When are vehicle tires considered unsafe?
question_official_en: When are vehicle tires considered to be unsafe?

options.text_original: Cuando el dibujo de las llantas es menor de 1mm de profundidad
options.text_translated: When the tread depth is less than 1mm
options.text_official_en: When the tire tread is less than 1mm in depth

options.text_original: Cuando el dibujo de las llantas está muy profundo
options.text_translated: When the tire tread is very deep
options.text_official_en: When the tire tread is very deep

options.text_original: Cuando las llantas están calientes por el sol y transitamos a 80 Km/h
options.text_translated: When the tires are hot from the sun and you are driving at 80 km/h
options.text_official_en: When the tires are hot from the sun and we drive at 80 km/h

explanation_en: Tires are considered unsafe when the tread depth falls below 1mm. Tread grooves are designed to channel water away and provide grip; when they wear down below 1mm, the tire loses its ability to grip the road, especially on wet surfaces. This dramatically increases stopping distance and the risk of hydroplaning.
explanation_es: Las llantas se consideran inseguras cuando la profundidad del dibujo cae por debajo de 1mm. Los surcos del dibujo están disenados para canalizar el agua y proporcionar adherencia; cuando se desgastan por debajo de 1mm, la llanta pierde su capacidad de agarre al camino, especialmente en superficies mojadas. Esto aumenta dramaticamente la distancia de frenado y el riesgo de aquaplaning.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SI AL CONDUCIR SU VEHÍCULO EN CARRETERA, LA LLANTA DELANTERA DERECHA LLEGARA A SALIR DEL PAVIMENTO ¿QUÉ DEBE USTED HACER?:
question_translated: If your front right tire goes off the pavement while driving on the highway, what should you do?
question_official_en: If while driving your vehicle on the highway, the front right tire leaves the pavement, what should you do?

options.text_original: Aplicar inmediatamente los frenos y volver al pavimento
options.text_translated: Hit the brakes immediately and return to the pavement
options.text_official_en: Immediately apply the brakes and return to the pavement

options.text_original: Sujetar con firmeza el volante, quitar el pie del acelerador y volver al pavimento
options.text_translated: Grip the steering wheel firmly, take your foot off the gas, and return to the pavement
options.text_official_en: Firmly grip the steering wheel, remove your foot from the accelerator and return to the pavement

options.text_original: Volver rápidamente al pavimento a su velocidad normal
options.text_translated: Quickly return to the pavement at your normal speed
options.text_official_en: Quickly return to the pavement at your normal speed

explanation_en: If your tire leaves the pavement, the key is to stay calm and maintain control. Grip the steering wheel firmly to prevent it from jerking, release the accelerator to slow down gradually, and then gently steer back onto the pavement. Braking suddenly or jerking the wheel at highway speed can cause you to overcorrect and lose control.
explanation_es: Si tu llanta sale del pavimento, la clave es mantener la calma y el control. Sujeta el volante con firmeza para evitar que gire bruscamente, suelta el acelerador para desacelerar gradualmente, y luego regresa suavemente al pavimento. Frenar repentinamente o girar el volante a velocidad de carretera puede causar una sobrecorrección y pérdida de control.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿QUÉ ENTENDEMOS POR MANEJO DEFENSIVO?:
question_translated: What is defensive driving?
question_official_en: What do we understand by defensive driving?

options.text_original: Conducir a muy baja velocidad
options.text_translated: Driving at very low speed
options.text_official_en: Driving at very low speed

options.text_original: Conducir un vehículo respetando las normas de vialidad para prevenir accidentes a pesar de los errores de los demás
options.text_translated: Driving while respecting traffic rules to prevent accidents despite the errors of others
options.text_official_en: Driving a vehicle respecting traffic regulations to prevent accidents despite the errors of others

options.text_original: Conducir a baja velocidad a pesar de los retrasos en los horarios
options.text_translated: Driving slowly despite schedule delays
options.text_official_en: Driving at low speed despite delays in schedules

explanation_en: Defensive driving means operating your vehicle while following all traffic laws AND anticipating the mistakes of other drivers. It is NOT simply driving slowly. A defensive driver stays alert, maintains safe following distances, and is prepared to react to the unpredictable actions of others to prevent accidents.
explanation_es: El manejo defensivo significa operar tu vehículo siguiendo todas las leyes de tránsito Y anticipando los errores de otros conductores. NO es simplemente conducir lento. Un conductor defensivo se mantiene alerta, mantiene distancias de seguimiento seguras y esta preparado para reaccionar ante las acciones impredecibles de otros para prevenir accidentes.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SI AL APROXIMARSE A UN CRUCERO EL COLOR DE LA LUZ DEL SEMÁFORO NO SE OBSERVA CLARO DEBIDO A LA ILUMINACIÓN DEL SOL, ¿QUÉ MEDIDAS DE SEGURIDAD DEBE DE REALIZAR?
question_translated: If you cannot clearly see the traffic light color due to sun glare as you approach an intersection, what should you do?
question_official_en: If upon approaching an intersection the color of the traffic light is not clearly observed due to the sun's illumination, what safety measures should you take?

options.text_original: Ceder el paso a los vehículos que transitan de Oriente a Poniente y esperar mi turno para cruzar
options.text_translated: Yield to vehicles traveling east to west and wait your turn to cross
options.text_official_en: Yield the right of way to vehicles traveling from east to west and wait my turn to cross

options.text_original: Disminuir la velocidad, observar la circulación de la calle que cruzaré además de su semáforo y, si me corresponde el siga, cruzar con precaución
options.text_translated: Slow down, observe the cross traffic and their traffic light, and if you have the green, proceed with caution
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed, observe the traffic on the street I will cross as well as their traffic light, and if I have the go signal, cross with caution

options.text_original: Pasar lo más rápido que se pueda, evitando chocar con los demás vehículos
options.text_translated: Pass through as quickly as possible, avoiding collisions with other vehicles
options.text_official_en: Pass as quickly as possible, avoiding collisions with other vehicles

explanation_en: When sun glare makes your traffic light hard to see, slow down and look at the cross street's traffic behavior and their signal instead. If they are stopped (meaning their light is red), your light is likely green, and you can proceed with caution. This indirect method of confirming your signal is much safer than guessing or speeding through.
explanation_es: Cuando el resplandor del sol dificulta ver su semáforo, reduzca la velocidad y observe el comportamiento del tráfico de la calle transversal y su señal. Si están detenidos (lo que significa que su luz es roja), su luz probablemente es verde y puedes proceder con precaución. Este metodo indirecto de confirmar su señal es mucho más seguro que adivinar o pasar a velocidad.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁLES SON LAS VENTAJAS DE REVISAR LLANTAS, BANDAS, LUCES Y NIVELES DE FLUIDOS CON LA PERIODICIDAD RECOMENDADA POR EL FABRICANTE?
question_translated: What are the benefits of regularly checking tires, belts, lights, and fluid levels as recommended by the manufacturer?
question_official_en: What are the advantages of checking tires, belts, lights and fluid levels with the frequency recommended by the manufacturer?

options.text_original: Evitar los accidentes con peatones
options.text_translated: To avoid accidents with pedestrians
options.text_official_en: To avoid accidents with pedestrians

options.text_original: Anticiparse a imprevistos, sobrecalentamiento y daño del motor, así como accidentes viales
options.text_translated: To anticipate problems, prevent overheating and engine damage, and prevent traffic accidents
options.text_official_en: To anticipate unforeseen events, overheating and engine damage, as well as traffic accidents

options.text_original: Se previenen conflictos viales y conductores arrollados
options.text_translated: To prevent traffic conflicts and drivers being run over
options.text_official_en: Traffic conflicts and run-over drivers are prevented

explanation_en: Regular maintenance checks help you catch problems before they become emergencies. Checking tires, belts, lights, and fluid levels as the manufacturer recommends prevents engine overheating, mechanical failures, and breakdowns that could lead to accidents. Preventive maintenance is always cheaper and safer than emergency repairs on the road.
explanation_es: Las revisiones regulares de mantenimiento te ayudan a detectar problemas antes de que se conviertan en emergencias. Revisar llantas, bandas, luces y niveles de fluidos como recomienda el fabricante previene el sobrecalentamiento del motor, fallas mecánicas y descomposturas que podrían provocar accidentes. El mantenimiento preventivo siempre es más económico y seguro que las reparaciones de emergencia en el camino.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿CUÁLES SON LAS OBLIGACIONES DE UN CONDUCTOR RESPONSABLE?
question_translated: What are the obligations of a responsible driver?
question_official_en: What are the obligations of a responsible driver?

options.text_original: Respetar los derechos y obligaciones de los demás usuarios de las vías públicas, así como las señales de tránsito e indicaciones de los Agente viales
options.text_translated: Respect the rights and obligations of all road users, and obey traffic signs and traffic officer instructions
options.text_official_en: Respect the rights and obligations of other public road users, as well as traffic signs and traffic officer instructions

options.text_original: Conducir con precaución, excediendo la velocidad solo cuando necesite hacerlo
options.text_translated: Drive cautiously, only exceeding the speed limit when necessary
options.text_official_en: Drive with caution, exceeding speed only when needed

options.text_original: Evitar accidentes al conducir, respetando el señalamiento vial a menos que por necesidad personal requiera no respetar los que considere menos importantes
options.text_translated: Avoid accidents while driving, respecting traffic signs unless personal need requires ignoring less important ones
options.text_official_en: Avoid accidents while driving, respecting traffic signs unless personal necessity requires not respecting those considered less important

explanation_en: A responsible driver must respect ALL traffic laws at ALL times -- there are no exceptions for personal convenience. This means obeying every traffic sign, following officer instructions, and respecting the rights of all road users including pedestrians, cyclists, and other drivers. Selective compliance is not an option.
explanation_es: Un conductor responsable debes respetar TODAS las leyes de tránsito en TODO momento -- no hay excepciones por conveniencia personal. Esto significa obedecer cada señal de tránsito, seguir las instrucciones de los agentes viales y respetar los derechos de todos los usuarios de la via pública, incluyendo peatones, ciclistas y otros conductores. El cumplimiento selectivo no es una opcion.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: DE LAS SIGUIENTES OPCIONES ¿CUÁL ES LA FORMA MÁS SEGURA PARA CAMBIAR UNA LLANTA PONCHADA?
question_translated: What is the safest way to change a flat tire?
question_official_en: Of the following options, which is the safest way to change a flat tire?

options.text_original: Estacionar el vehículo en una superficie plana, asegurar las llantas con un bloque de madera y cambiar la llanta como mejor se pueda
options.text_translated: Park on a flat surface, block the tires with a wooden block, and change the tire however you can
options.text_official_en: Park the vehicle on a flat surface, secure the tires with a wooden block and change the tire as best as possible

options.text_original: Evitar que el vehículo estorbe a los demás conductores, poner el freno de mano, asegurar las llantas con un bloque de madera después colocar en forma segura el gato, aflojar las tuercas de la llanta sólo media vuelta, subir el gato de tal forma que la llanta se eleve 3 cm, cambiar la llanta, bajar el gato y apretar fuerte las tuercas, quitar bloques de madera
options.text_translated: Move the vehicle out of the way, set the parking brake, block tires with a wooden block, place the jack securely, loosen the lug nuts only half a turn, raise the jack so the tire is 3cm off the ground, change the tire, lower the jack, tighten the nuts firmly, and remove the blocks
options.text_official_en: Ensure the vehicle does not obstruct other drivers, set the parking brake, secure the tires with a wooden block, then securely place the jack, loosen the wheel nuts only half a turn, raise the jack so that the tire is elevated 3cm, change the tire, lower the jack and firmly tighten the nuts, remove wooden blocks

options.text_original: Subir el vehículo preferentemente a la banqueta colocar el gato en cualquier punto del vehículo y subirlo lo más alto posible luego aflojar las tuercas deteniendo la llanta con los pies para evitar que de vueltas, cambiar la llanta, quitar gato y circular con seguridad
options.text_translated: Drive onto the sidewalk, place the jack anywhere on the vehicle, raise it as high as possible, loosen the nuts while holding the tire with your feet, change the tire, remove the jack, and drive safely
options.text_official_en: Preferably drive the vehicle onto the sidewalk, place the jack at any point on the vehicle and raise it as high as possible, then loosen the nuts while holding the tire with your feet to prevent it from spinning, change the tire, remove jack and drive safely

explanation_en: The correct procedure follows a specific safety sequence: first move the car out of traffic, engage the parking brake, and block the tires. Then place the jack at the proper reinforced point, loosen nuts only half a turn BEFORE lifting, raise just 3cm (enough to change the tire without instability), swap the tire, lower the jack, then fully tighten the nuts. Each step prevents a specific danger.
explanation_es: El procedimiento correcto sigue una secuencia de seguridad específica: primero mueva el auto fuera del tráfico, ponga el freno de mano y bloquee las llantas. Luego coloque el gato en el punto reforzado correcto, afloje las tuercas solo media vuelta ANTES de levantar, eleve solo 3cm (suficiente para cambiar la llanta sin inestabilidad), cambie la llanta, baje el gato y luego apriete completamente las tuercas. Cada paso previene un peligro específico.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SI CUANDO NOS SUBIMOS A NUESTRO VEHÍCULO EL PARABRISAS ESTÁ SUCIO, ES RECOMENDABLE:
question_translated: If your windshield is dirty when you get in your vehicle, what should you do?
question_official_en: If when we get into our vehicle the windshield is dirty, it is recommended:

options.text_original: Circular a alta velocidad para limpiarlo y así conduciremos más seguros
options.text_translated: Drive at high speed to clean it off, so you can drive more safely
options.text_official_en: Drive at high speed to clean it and thus we will drive more safely

options.text_original: Limpiarlo de preferencia con agua corriente y un poco de jabón
options.text_translated: Clean it preferably with running water and a little soap
options.text_official_en: Clean it preferably with running water and a little soap

options.text_original: Utilizar los limpiabrisas en seco ya que evitamos que se haga lodo con la tierra o el polvo
options.text_translated: Use the windshield wipers dry since that avoids making mud with the dirt or dust
options.text_official_en: Use the windshield wipers dry since we avoid making mud with the dirt or dust

explanation_en: A dirty windshield should be cleaned with running water and soap before driving. Using dry wipers on a dusty windshield creates a muddy smear that makes visibility even worse and can scratch the glass. Driving at high speed hoping the wind will clean it is reckless and keeps you driving blind in the meantime.
explanation_es: Un parabrisas sucio se debe limpiar con agua corriente y jabón antes de manejar. Usar los limpiabrisas en seco sobre un parabrisas polvoriento crea una mancha lodosa que empeora la visibilidad y puede rayar el vidrio. Manejar a alta velocidad esperando que el viento lo limpie es imprudente y te mantiene manejando a ciegas mientras tanto.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: ¿ES NECESARIO AJUSTAR LOS ESPEJOS RETROVISORES DEL VEHICULO A NUESTRA ALTURA SI VAMOS A CIRCULAR SOLAMENTE 1 KM?
question_translated: Is it necessary to adjust your rearview mirrors even if you are only driving 1 km?
question_official_en: Is it necessary to adjust the rearview mirrors of the vehicle to our height if we are going to drive only 1 km?

options.text_original: No es necesarios en tramos cortos
options.text_translated: Not necessary for short trips
options.text_official_en: It is not necessary on short distances

options.text_original: Realmente no es importante
options.text_translated: It really does not matter
options.text_official_en: It really is not important

options.text_original: Sí es necesario por seguridad sin importar la distancia
options.text_translated: Yes, it is necessary for safety regardless of the distance
options.text_official_en: Yes, it is necessary for safety regardless of the distance

explanation_en: You must ALWAYS adjust your mirrors before driving, no matter how short the trip. Accidents can happen at any distance -- even within 1 km of your starting point. Properly adjusted mirrors eliminate blind spots and give you full awareness of your surroundings, which is essential for every single drive.
explanation_es: Debes SIEMPRE ajustar tus espejos antes de manejar, sin importar qué tan corto sea el trayecto. Los accidentes pueden ocurrir a cualquier distancia -- incluso dentro de 1 km de tu punto de partida. Los espejos correctamente ajustados eliminan los puntos ciegos y te dan plena conciencia de tu entorno, lo cual es esencial para cada viaje.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: SI AL APROXIMARNOS A UN CRUCERO NO SE VE CLARAMENTE EL SEMÁFORO YA QUE ANUNCIOS, ÁRBOLES U OTROS OBJETOS ME IMPIDEN VERLO CON CLARIDAD, SE RECOMIENDA POR SEGURIDAD HACER LO SIGUIENTE:
question_translated: If you cannot clearly see the traffic light because signs, trees, or other objects are blocking it as you approach an intersection, what should you do?
question_official_en: If when approaching an intersection the traffic light is not clearly visible as signs, trees or other objects prevent seeing it clearly, it is recommended for safety to do the following:

options.text_original: Acelerar y tocar el claxon para que los otros conductores me cedan el paso
options.text_translated: Speed up and honk so other drivers let you through
options.text_official_en: Accelerate and honk the horn so that the other drivers yield the right of way

options.text_original: Dejar de acelerar y pasar despacio
options.text_translated: Stop accelerating and pass through slowly
options.text_official_en: Stop accelerating and pass slowly

options.text_original: Disminuir velocidad, llegar con precaución al cruce procurando ver el semáforo de más cerca y observar detenidamente hacia ambos lados evitando cualquier problema
options.text_translated: Slow down, approach the intersection cautiously trying to see the traffic light up close, and look carefully both ways to avoid any problems
options.text_official_en: Reduce speed, arrive with caution at the intersection trying to see the traffic light from closer, and carefully observe both sides avoiding any problem

explanation_en: When your view of the traffic light is blocked, you must slow down and approach cautiously. Try to see the signal from a closer vantage point, and look both ways to check for cross traffic. Simply coasting through slowly is not enough -- you need to actively verify the signal state and check for hazards before proceeding.
explanation_es: Cuando su vista del semáforo esta bloqueada, debes disminuir la velocidad y acercarse con precaución. Intente ver la señal desde un punto más cercano y mire a ambos lados para verificar el tráfico transversal. Simplemente pasar lentamente no es suficiente -- necesita verificar activamente el estado de la señal y revisar si hay peligros antes de proceder.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: AL CIRCULAR DURANTE LA NOCHE EN CONDICIONES OPTIMAS DE CLIMA:
question_translated: When driving at night under optimal weather conditions:
question_official_en: When driving at night under optimal climate conditions:

options.text_original: Podemos distinguir a un peatón con ropa obscura a 100 metros de distancia
options.text_translated: We can spot a pedestrian wearing dark clothing at 100 meters
options.text_official_en: We can distinguish a pedestrian with dark clothing at 100 meters distance

options.text_original: Es difícil ver a los peatones a más de 20 metros, por eso se debe de extremar precauciones al circular por zonas urbanas y/o donde haya concentraciones de peatones
options.text_translated: It is difficult to see pedestrians beyond 20 meters, so extra caution is needed in urban areas and where pedestrians gather
options.text_official_en: It is difficult to see pedestrians at more than 20 meters, which is why extra precautions should be taken when driving in urban zones and/or where there are concentrations of pedestrians

options.text_original: Podemos ver a un peatón vestido con ropa amarilla desde una distancia de 80 m
options.text_translated: We can see a pedestrian wearing yellow clothing from 80 meters away
options.text_official_en: We can see a pedestrian dressed in yellow clothing from a distance of 80 m

explanation_en: Even under optimal weather conditions at night, it is extremely difficult to see pedestrians beyond 20 meters. Headlights have limited range and pedestrians in dark clothing are nearly invisible. This is why you must exercise extra caution in urban areas and anywhere pedestrians may be present, especially near crosswalks and bus stops.
explanation_es: Incluso en condiciones optimas de clima durante la noche, es extremadamente difícil ver a los peatones a más de 20 metros. Las luces delanteras tienen alcance limitado y los peatones con ropa oscura son casi invisibles. Por eso debes tener precaución adicional en zonas urbanas y donde haya peatones presentes, especialmente cerca de cruces peatonales y paradas de autobus.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte

question_original: EL USAR AUDÍFONOS PARA ESCUCHAR MÚSICA MIENTRAS CONDUCE ES:
question_translated: Using earphones to listen to music while driving is:
question_official_en: Using earphones to listen to music while driving is:

options.text_original: Peligroso solamente para quien los usa
options.text_translated: Dangerous only for the person using them
options.text_official_en: Dangerous only for the person who uses them

options.text_original: Peligroso sólo para los peatones
options.text_translated: Dangerous only for pedestrians
options.text_official_en: Dangerous only for pedestrians

options.text_original: Peligroso para todos los usuarios de la vía pública cercanos a quien los usa
options.text_translated: Dangerous for ALL nearby road users
options.text_official_en: Dangerous for all users of the public road near the person who uses them

explanation_en: Wearing earphones while driving is dangerous for EVERYONE nearby -- not just the driver or just pedestrians. Earphones block critical auditory cues like horns, sirens, and the sounds of approaching vehicles. This impairs the driver's ability to react to emergencies, putting all surrounding road users at risk including passengers, pedestrians, cyclists, and other drivers.
explanation_es: Usar audifonos mientras conduce es peligroso para TODOS los que están cerca -- no solo para el conductor o solo para los peatones. Los audifonos bloquean señales auditivas criticas como bocinas, sirenas y los sonidos de vehículos que se aproximan. Esto deteriora la capacidad del conductor para reaccionar ante emergencias, poniendo en riesgo a todos los usuarios de la via cercanos, incluyendo pasajeros, peatones, ciclistas y otros conductores.
source: Gobierno Del Estado de Jalisco - Secretaría de Vialidad y Transporte
